texto: There is a deeper, more profound reason for this craving for acceptance and glory. Put simply, it's because all writers are fat and/or ugly. And generally socially inept. Me being the notable exception, of course. Writers want to be special, because they're so not. They're losers, overgrown kids who've never escaped from being misfits and who have run away into their own imaginations in an attempt to find self-esteem. Why do you think they all star in their own books? Self included.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Chancery Stone que provoca limites e escolhas conscientes. Ao questionar “There is a deeper, more profound reason for this craving for acceptance and glory. Put simply, it…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre autoestima, chancery-stone, danny, com foco em danny e writers.
